description:
Load the SARS-CoV-2 Mpro structure (7VH8) and its electron density map (7vh8_2fofc.ccp4) into PyMOL. Focus on Chain A. Identify the nirmatrelvir ligand (the main non-standard organic molecule in the active site) and note its 3-letter PDB code. Identify the active site catalytic residue that forms a covalent bond with the ligand's warhead. Create an isomesh named `ligand_density` from the loaded map, covering the nirmatrelvir ligand in Chain A at a contour level of exactly 1.0 sigma. Display the ligand and the catalytic cysteine in sticks representation, while the rest of the protein can be shown as a cartoon. Render and save a high-resolution PNG image to /home/ga/PyMOL_Data/images/paxlovid_density.png. Write a structural analysis report to /home/ga/PyMOL_Data/paxlovid_density_report.txt containing:
- The 3-letter PDB code of the nirmatrelvir ligand.
- The residue name and number of the catalytic residue covalently bound to the ligand.
- The contour level of the isomesh used (1.0).
- The measured distance (in Angstroms) between the sulfur atom (SG) of the catalytic cysteine and the covalent attachment point (carbon atom) on the ligand.
